instrument_name: Spotify Discovery Intent & Control Validation Survey
owner_note: Create the Google Form only after the wording and response options are approved. Do not populate charts with synthetic responses.
research_decision: Should Artifact B continue from an interaction prototype into a Spotify-connected pilot for active discovery seekers?
objectives: Measure how often active Spotify listeners intentionally seek unfamiliar music and where the journey fails.
Separate relevance, repetition, control, and context-contamination failure modes.
Test willingness to provide one short intent signal before recommendations.
Identify the most valuable control for a Discovery Compass prototype.
Recruit participants for a paired baseline-versus-Compass usability study.

target_sample.target_segment: Spotify listeners who intentionally seek new-to-them music at least monthly
recruitment_mix: Existing personal network and music communities
Artifact A visitors who opt in
Relevant Reddit or Discord communities where posting research is permitted
target_sample.interpretation_limit: Convenience recruitment is directional product discovery, not a population estimate of all Spotify users.

form_settings.privacy_copy: This research asks about music-discovery behavior. Do not enter account credentials, private playlist links, or sensitive personal information. Results will be analysed in aggregate.

questions.prompt: Are you at least 18 and willing to take part in this product-research survey?
How often do you currently use Spotify?
options: I do not currently use Spotify

questions.prompt: How often do you intentionally look for music or artists that are new to you?
options: Two or three times a month

questions.prompt: Where do you usually look for new-to-you music? Select all that apply.
options: Music blogs, radio, or live events

sections.title: The last discovery attempt
sections.intro: Think about the most recent time you deliberately tried to find music that was new to you.

questions.prompt: Can you remember a specific attempt from the last four weeks?
What were you trying to find, and what did you do first?
What was the main outcome of that attempt?
options: I saved or replayed several discoveries
I switched to another source or service

questions.prompt: What was the single biggest problem, if any?
options: Recommendations did not fit what I wanted
The same tracks or artists appeared again
Suggestions were too similar to what I already know
Suggestions were too unfamiliar or risky
I could not tell the system what I wanted
Old or shared listening distorted the results

questions.prompt: How satisfied were you with that discovery attempt?
When Spotify recommends music, how often do these things happen?
rows: The recommendation does not fit my current mood or activity
I hear the same tracks or artists again
The results feel too similar to what I already know
Old, shared, sleep, or background listening affects recommendations
I leave Spotify to find ideas elsewhere

questions.prompt: Which workarounds have you used? Select all that apply.
options: Searched with a specific artist, track, or genre
Used another app or website for discovery
I have not used a workaround

questions.prompt: How much control do you feel you have over Spotify's discovery recommendations today?
If discovery repeatedly disappoints you, what are you most likely to do?
options: Keep using Spotify but stop actively exploring
Use Spotify only for known music
Find recommendations elsewhere and return to Spotify to play them
Use another music service more often

sections.title: Discovery Compass concept
sections.concept_card: Imagine a discovery tool where you choose a few music anchors, describe what fits right now, approve how the tool understood you, and set how adventurous the results should be. It shows 8–12 recommendations with short reasons and updates immediately when you save, reject, or refine.

questions.prompt: Would you spend about 20 seconds giving this intent before seeing recommendations?
Which single control would be most valuable to you?
options: Genres, artists, or contexts to exclude
Mood, activity, or energy for this session
A reset that ignores selected listening history
Immediate 'more like this' and 'less like this' feedback

questions.prompt: How likely is this concept to help you find relevant new-to-you music?
How much effort does this concept sound like?
What level of novelty would you choose most often?
What would concern you about this concept? Select all that apply.
options: I do not want to explain my taste
It may still repeat familiar music
It may miss the music I already love

questions.prompt: What is the one change that would make this concept more useful to you?
Would you be willing to try two short discovery experiences and discuss the difference for 20–25 minutes?
If you answered Yes or Maybe, leave an email for research scheduling.
questions.privacy_note: Keep contact information in a separate response sheet from analysis exports.

chart_plan.message_tested: A short intent step is acceptable to enough target users.
chart_plan.reporting_rule: Show n and all three options; do not combine 'It depends' with 'Yes'.

chart_plan.message_tested: One control clearly leads the feature-value hierarchy.
chart_plan.reporting_rule: Use direct labels; if no option exceeds 35%, describe demand as fragmented.

chart_plan.message_tested: The dominant recalled failure aligns with the Artifact A mechanism.
chart_plan.reporting_rule: Exclude 'No meaningful problem' from failure ranking but show it separately.

continue_to_paired_test: At least 60% of qualified respondents answer Yes or It depends to Q14, with Yes reported separately.
Median concept value on Q16 is at least 4/5.
Median perceived effort on Q17 is no more than 3/5.
At least one of the top three recalled failure modes is directly addressed by the prototype.
revise_before_test: Setup effort is a top-two concern or Q17 median exceeds 3/5.
No control in Q15 reaches 35%, indicating the proposition needs sharper segmentation.
Privacy/account access dominates concerns, requiring clearer no-login positioning.
stop_or_reframe: Fewer than 40% answer Yes or It depends to Q14.
Median concept value is below 3/5 among monthly-or-more discovery seekers.

analysis_rules: Report the qualified respondent count on every chart.
Cut results by discovery frequency; do not over-index on aggregate averages.
Treat Q05–Q09 as stronger behavioral evidence than general opinions because they reference a recent event.
Keep open-text coding separate from the fixed Artifact A taxonomy until two reviewers agree on merges.
Do not claim statistical significance from the initial convenience sample.
Do not infer Spotify-wide prevalence from this survey or Artifact A.
